sourceRef: tip:How to name files so your audit self thanks you

slides.headline: Maximize audit readiness
slides.body: Keep your VA school files organized for future audits.

slides.headline: Use the format
slides.body: Always use the same three tokens: [Year]-[Month]-[Day]

slides.headline: Include key information
slides.body: Add relevant details: [Student ID]-[Course Code]-[Document Type]

slides.headline: Review and adjust
slides.body: Regularly check and update your file naming convention.

slides.headline: Your audit will thank you
slides.body: Files named correctly make audits smoother and more efficient.
bullets: Example: 'Ensure all student records are correctly labeled'

slides.headline: Maintain compliance
slides.body: Regularly reviewing and adjusting your file naming helps with ongoing compliance.
bullets: Compliance is a journey, not a destination

slides.body: Start now: Begin implementing your new file naming convention today.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Maximize audit readiness by following these three simple steps: [Year]-[Month]-[Day] format, include key information, and review regularly. Your audit self will thank you.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is the first one, as it could benefit from a more compelling hook to engage the audience.
